---
title: "Week 7: Text Classification"
subtitle: "EDS 231 — Spring 2026"
date: "May 5, 2026"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: bootstrap
    highlight: pygments
    css: week-styles.css
    toc: false
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

> Using supervised machine learning to classify text documents — predicting outcomes from language with Naive Bayes and regularized regression.

---

### Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Explain the generative assumptions behind Naive Bayes classification
- Build a text classification pipeline using `{tidymodels}` and `{textrecipes}`
- Apply tf-idf feature engineering as a preprocessing step for classification
- Evaluate model performance using accuracy, ROC AUC, and confusion matrices
- Tune a Lasso regularization penalty and interpret the resulting word-importance coefficients

---

### Recommended Reading

📖 [Text Mining with R — Ch. 7: Case Study — Comparing Twitter Archives](https://www.tidytextmining.com/twitter)

📖 [Hvitfeldt & Silge — Supervised Machine Learning for Text Analysis in R](https://smltar.com/)

---

---

## Lecture Materials

### Slides

🖥 Slide deck from this week's lecture

[View Slides](#https://ucsb.box.com/s/xjc9a7fhvos9h0gncaboz1arofhvz6vn){.btn .btn-primary}

### Code & Data

💻 R scripts used during class:

[Lab5](https://github.com/MaRo406/EDS-231-text-sentiment/blob/main/Labs/Lab5.Rmd)

---

## 📂 Assignment

### Lab 5 — Text Classification <span class="badge badge-danger">Graded · 11 pts</span>

**Due: May 19 @ 11:59 pm**

In this lab you will build a supervised text classifier to predict whether a climbing incident report describes a fatal or non-fatal accident. You will fit a Naive Bayes baseline, then compare its performance to other models on held-out test data.

[Download Lab 5](https://github.com/MaRo406/EDS-231-text-sentiment/blob/main/Labs/Lab5.Rmd){.btn .btn-primary}

---

---

## 📚 Text Classification in Environmental Science — Key Citations

Curated readings organized by thematic area. Useful background for situating the classification methods from this week in applied environmental research contexts.

<details>
<summary><strong>Climate Change & Misinformation</strong></summary>
<table>
<thead><tr><th>Citation</th><th>Year</th><th>Topic</th><th>Keywords</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td><a href="https://www.nature.com/articles/s41558-021-01143-3">Coan et al. — <em>Nature Climate Change</em></a></td><td>2021</td><td>Climate Denial Detection</td><td>contrarian claims, binary classification, SVM, misinformation, media framing</td></tr>
<tr><td><a href="https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10540966/">Shyrokykh et al. — <em>PLOS ONE</em></a></td><td>2023</td><td>Climate Tweet Classification</td><td>short text, Naive Bayes, SVM, BERT, Twitter, climate relevance</td></tr>
<tr><td><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.12010">Webersinke et al. — <em>arXiv</em></a></td><td>2021</td><td>ClimateBERT</td><td>domain-adapted language model, climate-relevant text detection, transfer learning</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Biodiversity & Conservation</strong></summary>
<table>
<thead><tr><th>Citation</th><th>Year</th><th>Topic</th><th>Keywords</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td><a href="https://besjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/2041-210X.13608">Kulkarni et al. — <em>Methods in Ecology &amp; Evolution</em></a></td><td>2021</td><td>Threatened Species News Filtering</td><td>binary classification, CITES, news relevance, random forest, information retrieval</td></tr>
<tr><td><a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.05133">Grasso et al. — <em>arXiv</em></a></td><td>2024</td><td>EcoVerse Dataset</td><td>eco-relevance, annotated Twitter dataset, binary classification benchmark</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>Environmental Health & Pollution</strong></summary>
<table>
<thead><tr><th>Citation</th><th>Year</th><th>Topic</th><th>Keywords</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td><a href="https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0173132">Patel et al. — <em>PLOS ONE</em></a></td><td>2017</td><td>Exposure Assessment Literature Screening</td><td>text mining, PubMed, binary relevance classification, systematic review automation</td></tr>
<tr><td><a href="https://bmcpublichealth.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12889-017-4914-3">Bellinger et al. — <em>BMC Public Health</em></a></td><td>2017</td><td>Air Pollution Epidemiology Screening</td><td>ML, data mining, systematic review, air pollution, study relevance classification</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>
</details>

---

[← Week 6: LDA Topic Modeling](week-6.html)
